rubric.criteria: Given a broad multi-document comparison task, judge whether the tool strategy shown in the timeline is appropriate to that breadth (a multi-pronged investigation — e.g. delegating per-vendor research — rather than a single shallow lookup), and whether the final answer actually compares both named vendors against the draft terms.

packet.expectations: The user asked for a broad comparison of two vendor proposals across several dimensions (price, service level, liability), each checked against the draft contract terms — a task whose breadth warrants investigating each vendor/dimension (e.g. delegating per-vendor research) rather than one lookup. Verify from the timeline whether the strategy was appropriate to that breadth, and whether the answer compares both named vendors against the draft terms.

steps.summary:
[Vendor-Proposal-Brightpath.txt (1 pages) — full text]

Brightpath Freight — Proposal. Annual fee: three hundred and ninety thousand pounds (£390,000). Service level: 98.0% on-time delivery, measured quarterly.

Liability. Brightpath's aggregate liability is capped at fifty percent (50%) of the annual fee. Brightpath requires the Buyer to indemnify it against third-party claims arising from the Buyer's instructions.

steps.name: read_document
steps.summary:
[RFQ-Instructions.txt (1 pages) — full text]

1. Request for Quotation. Helix Manufacturing Limited (the "Buyer") invites quotations for the supply of managed logistics services. Quotations must be submitted by no later than 5:00pm on 30 September 2026. Late submissions will not be considered.

2. Evaluation Criteria. Quotations will be evaluated on price (40%), service levels (35%), and contractual risk including liability terms (25%). The Buyer is not bound to accept the lowest or any quotation.

steps.name: read_document
steps.summary:
[Vendor-Proposal-Northstar.txt (1 pages) — full text]

Northstar Logistics — Proposal. Annual fee: four hundred and twenty thousand pounds (£420,000). Service level: 99.5% on-time delivery, measured monthly.

Liability. Northstar's aggregate liability is capped at one hundred percent (100%) of the annual fee. Northstar excludes all liability for indirect and consequential loss.

steps.name: read_document
steps.summary:
[Draft-MSA-Terms.txt (1 pages) — full text]

Draft Contract Terms. The Buyer's standard terms require a liability cap of at least one hundred and fifty percent (150%) of the annual fee and prohibit any Buyer-side indemnity of the supplier.

Governing Law. The Buyer's standard terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales. Any service level below 99.0% on-time delivery requires director-level approval.

{"content_md": "# Helix Logistics RFQ — Vendor Evaluation

**Client (Buyer):** Helix Manufacturing Limited  
**Subject:** Managed logistics services — RFQ evaluation  

## Documents in Play

| Document | Key Content |
|---|---|
| RFQ-Instructions.txt | RFQ issued by Helix Manufacturing Ltd. Evaluation: Price 40%, Service 35%, Contractual Risk 25%. Deadline 30 Sep 2026. |
| Draft-MSA-Terms.txt | Buyer's standard terms: liability cap ≥150% of annual fee; no Buyer-side indemnity of supplier; England & Wales governing law; <99.0% on-time delivery requires director-level approval. |
| Vendor-Proposal-Brightpath.txt | £390,000/yr; 98.0% on-time (quarterly); liability cap at 50% of annual fee; demands Buyer indemnify Brightpath. |
| Vendor-Proposal-Northstar.txt | £420,000/yr; 99.5% on-time (monthly); liability cap at 100% of annual fee; excludes all indirect/consequential loss. |

## Evaluation Against Buyer's Standard Terms

See working analysis below.
"}

steps.name: update_matter_memory
steps.summary: Updated this matter's memory (945 characters). It is saved and will be available in future runs on this matter; the prior version was snapshotted so the change can be undone.

---

## Helix Logistics RFQ — Comprehensive Vendor Evaluation

### 1. The RFQ Framework (RFQ-Instructions.txt)

The Buyer (Helix Manufacturing Ltd) asks for managed logistics quotations evaluated on:
- **Price — 40%**
- **Service levels — 35%**
- **Contractual risk (incl. liability terms) — 25%**

---

### 2. Price Comparison

| Vendor | Annual Fee | Price Rank |
|---|---|---|
| **Brightpath Freight** | **£390,000** | Lower (£30k cheaper) |
| **Northstar Logistics** | **£420,000** | Higher |

*Source: Vendor-Proposal-Brightpath.txt (p1), Vendor-Proposal-Northstar.txt (p1)*

**Brightpath wins on price by £30,000/yr (7.1% cheaper).**

---

### 3. Service Level Comparison

| Vendor | On-Time Delivery | Measurement Period | Against Our Floor |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Brightpath Freight** | **98.0%** | **Quarterly** | **Below 99.0% floor** — requires director-level approval per Draft-MSA-Terms.txt (p1) |
| **Northstar Logistics** | **99.5%** | **Monthly** | **Above 99.0% floor** — no special approval needed |

*Sources: Vendor-Proposal-Brightpath.txt (p1), Vendor-Proposal-Northstar.txt (p1), Draft-MSA-Terms.txt (p1)*

**Northstar wins on service quality:** not only is 99.5% > 98.0%, but monthly measurement is more responsive than quarterly — problems surface faster.

**Key risk — Brightpath:** 98.0% on-time is below our own documented floor of 99.0% (Draft-MSA-Terms.txt, p1). This would require director-level approval to proceed. Even if that is obtained, a 1 percentage point gap means ~7.3 days of late deliveries per year vs ~1.8 days for Northstar (assuming daily shipments).

---

### 4. Liability & Contractual Risk Comparison

| Term | Buyer's Standard | Brightpath | Northstar |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Liability cap** | **≥150%** of annual fee | **50%** of annual fee | **100%** of annual fee |
| **Buyer-side indemnity of supplier** | **Prohibited** | **Demanded** — Buyer must indemnify Brightpath | **Not demanded** — compliant |
| **Indirect/consequential exclusion** | N…

packet.visible_answer:
---

## Helix Logistics RFQ — Comprehensive Vendor Evaluation

### 1. The RFQ Framework (RFQ-Instructions.txt)

The Buyer (Helix Manufacturing Ltd) asks for managed logistics quotations evaluated on:
- **Price — 40%**
- **Service levels — 35%**
- **Contractual risk (incl. liability terms) — 25%**

---

### 2. Price Comparison

| Vendor | Annual Fee | Price Rank |
|---|---|---|
| **Brightpath Freight** | **£390,000** | Lower (£30k cheaper) |
| **Northstar Logistics** | **£420,000** | Higher |

*Source: Vendor-Proposal-Brightpath.txt (p1), Vendor-Proposal-Northstar.txt (p1)*

**Brightpath wins on price by £30,000/yr (7.1% cheaper).**

---

### 3. Service Level Comparison

| Vendor | On-Time Delivery | Measurement Period | Against Our Floor |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Brightpath Freight** | **98.0%** | **Quarterly** | **Below 99.0% floor** — requires director-level approval per Draft-MSA-Terms.txt (p1) |
| **Northstar Logistics** | **99.5%** | **Monthly** | **Above 99.0% floor** — no special approval needed |

*Sources: Vendor-Proposal-Brightpath.txt (p1), Vendor-Proposal-Northstar.txt (p1), Draft-MSA-Terms.txt (p1)*

**Northstar wins on service quality:** not only is 99.5% > 98.0%, but monthly measurement is more responsive than quarterly — problems surface faster.

**Key risk — Brightpath:** 98.0% on-time is below our own documented floor of 99.0% (Draft-MSA-Terms.txt, p1). This would require director-level approval to proceed. Even if that is obtained, a 1 percentage point gap means ~7.3 days of late deliveries per year vs ~1.8 days for Northstar (assuming daily shipments).

---

### 4. Liability & Contractual Risk Comparison

| Term | Buyer's Standard | Brightpath | Northstar |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Liability cap** | **≥150%** of annual fee | **50%** of annual fee | **100%** of annual fee |
| **Buyer-side indemnity of supplier** | **Prohibited** | **Demanded** — Buyer must indemnify Brightpath | **Not demanded** — compliant |
| **Indirect/consequential exclusion** | Not specified in draft terms | Not mentioned | **Demanded** — all indirect/consequential loss excluded |
| **Governing law** | England & Wales | (not stated) | (not stated) |

*Sources: Draft-MSA-Terms.txt (p1), Vendor-Proposal-Brightpath.txt (p1), Vendor-Proposal-Northstar.txt (p1)*

#### Brightpath — Liability Risks (Critical)

1. **Cap at 50% of annual fee** — that is **3× below** our minimum floor of 150%. Even in a major service failure, our recovery is severely limited.
2. **Buyer indemnity demanded** — directly violates our Draft-MSA-Terms prohibition. If Brightpath is sued by a third party due to our instructions, *we* pay their defence and damages. This is the most aggressive risk shift in either proposal.

#### Northstar — Liability Risks (Material)

1. **Cap at 100% of annual fee** — below our 150% floor, but only by a third. This is bridgeable through negotiation (pro-rate to 150% or £420k×150%=£630k).
2. **Indirect/consequential exclusion** — standard in logistics but a risk to flag. It would bar recovery for things like production-line downtime caused by late deliveries. Our draft terms don't address this head-on, so it's a gap to negotiate (carve back losses for IP breach, confidentiality breach, or wilful default).

#### Neither proposal addresses governing law — both need to adopt England & Wales (per Draft-MSA-Terms.txt, p1).

---

### 5. Summary — Two-Party Scorecard

| Criterion | Weight | Brightpath Freight | Northstar Logistics |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Price** | 40% | **✓** £390k | — £420k (+£30k) |
| **Service level** | 35% | **✗** 98.0% quarterly | **✓** 99.5% monthly |
| **Contractual risk** | 25% | **✗✗** Cap @ 50% + Buyer indemnity demanded | **—** Cap @ 100% + indirect exclusion (negotiable) |

---

### 6. Key Risks Flagged

| # | Risk | Vendor | Severity | What to Do |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Liability cap at 50%** (vs 150% floor) | Brightpath | **Critical** | Cannot accept — cap is 3× below floor. Escalate for walk-away or demand minimum 150%. |
| 2 | **Buyer indemnity of supplier** (flatly prohibited) | Brightpath | **Critical** | Cannot accept — violates a hard prohibition. Reject entirely. |
| 3 | **Service level below 99.0% floor** | Brightpath | **Material** | Requires director-level approval even to consider. |
| 4 | **Liability cap at 100%** (vs 150% floor) | Northstar | **Material** | Negotiate up to 150% of annual fee (£630k). |
| 5 | **Indirect/consequential loss exclusion** | Northstar | **Material** | Add carve-backs for IP breach, confidentiality breach, and wilful default. |
| 6 | **Neither vendor states governing law** | Both | **Minor** | Confirm both agree to England & Wales. |

---

### 7. Initial Recommendation

**Northstar Logistics** is the stronger candidate overall despite being £30k more expensive. Its service level (99.5%, monthly) exceeds our floor, its liability cap (100%) is much closer to our 150% target, and it does not demand a Buyer-side indemnity. The main negotiating items are the cap (push to 150%) and the indirect-loss exclusion (add standard carve-backs).

**Brightpath Freight** carries two critical risks — a 50% cap and a demanded Buyer indemnity — that hit the two hard prohibitions in our Draft-MSA-Terms. Even if the price is attractive, the contractual gap is very wide. Engaging Brightpath would require major concessions from them on both liability points, and the service level would need director-level sign-off.

---

**Items requiring human judgment:**
1. **Director-level approval** — if the business wants to pursue Brightpath despite its 98.0% SLA, that requires director sign-off per Draft-MSA-Terms.
2. **Negotiation mandate** — are we authorised to negotiate caps up from 100% (Northstar) and 50% (Brightpath), or is the 150% floor a hard walk-away?
3. **Brightpath's Buyer indemnity** — this violates a hard prohibition. If the business wants Brightpath anyway, this needs to be escalated.

verdict.text:
VERDICT: PASS
appropriate_strategy: yes
both_compared: yes
EVIDENCE: "Brightpath Freight ... 98.0% on-time ... Below 99.0% floor — requires director-level approval per Draft-MSA-Terms.txt (p1) ... Northstar Logistics ... 99.5% ... Above 99.0% floor — no special approval needed ... Brightpath — Liability cap at 50% of annual fee — that is 3× below our minimum floor of 150%. Buyer indemnity demanded — directly violates our Draft-MSA-Terms prohibition ... Northstar — Cap at 100% of annual fee — below our 150% floor, but only by a third."

- Multi-pronged strategy: agent read all four relevant documents (RFQ, draft terms, both vendor proposals) before comparing.
- The answer explicitly weighs each vendor against the draft terms on price, service level, liability cap, buyer indemnity, indirect exclusion, and governing law.
- Every quantitative and qualitative claim (e.g., £390k, 98.0%, 50% cap, buyer indemnity prohibition) matches the exact text retrieved in the timeline; no unsupported assertions.
